priorities.label: S96 P1 — NONET living-realm VIDEO upgrade: veo image-to-video character loops + dusk backdrop replace the procedural sprite twitch
priorities.objective: Replace the procedural breathe/blink/hop/float sprite twitch (user: 'looks like an early-2000s PowerPoint shape spinning') with REAL veo-generated video that brings the 4 original NONET characters to life — moving + doing interesting things — at high quality.
priorities.scope: Pipeline: transparent sprite -> dark dusk keyframe (Pillow) -> veo-3.1 image-to-video -> ffmpeg seamless xfade-loop + soft-vignette + opaque VP9 -> Pixi video texture, feathered alpha mask, video-primary/static-fallback, paused when hidden. Plus a dusk-forest ambient backdrop loop. OUT: procedural spirit reactions (user de-prioritised), IP cleanup, Voltkin.
priorities.testing: tsc 0; full vitest green (overlay not node-tested; no import-time break); build + bundle guard pass (entry flat 553.6/560 — video code lazy, assets off-bundle); LIVE preview: 4 spirit videos + backdrop load (readyState 4), play, advance + loop seamlessly, masked composite over the board, board legible, 0 console errors, procedural spirits list empties (video won all 4).
priorities.check_method: S96 P1: NONET characters brought to life with real video. Pipeline (all proven end-to-end before fan-out): (1) Pillow composites each approved transparent sprite (kami/owl-a/owl-b/moss-b, 512^2 RGBA) onto a dark dusk-forest keyframe so veo has a rich opaque seed whose dark bg later melts into the overlay's dark backdrop; (2) veo-3.1-fast image-to-video, 9:16 720p 6s, personality-tailored motion prompt per character (elder breathes/blinks/lantern-flicker; owl-a head-turn+blink; owl-b peppy bounce+wing-flap; moss-b dreamy float) + an 8s 16:9 dusk-forest ambient backdrop (text-to-video); (3) ffmpeg seamless loop via xfade-against-self at offset=(D-CF) keeping the middle [CF,D] segment (first frame == last frame; measured seam diff mean 1.4/255), + mild vignette, stripped audio, opaque VP9 (libvpx alpha plane is broken in this build — both VP8/VP9 dropped it; alphamerge verified working pre-encode, so pivoted to render-time masking). Compositing in Pixi: manual HTMLVideoElement + Texture.from (explicit play/pause refs, muted->autoplay-allowed), each video sprite gets a feathered radial alpha-mask Sprite (spirit-mask.png), all in a dedicated realmLayer added before the board frame so async sprites render BEHIND the grid; backdrop at alpha 0.42; videos play on show / pause on hide, loop currentTimes desynced. Video is the PRIMARY layer over the S95 static-webp + procedural idle FALLBACK (whichever wins the load race wins; a guardian's procedural entry is dropped from this.spirits when its video loads -> animateLife never twitches a video). GATES: tsc 0; vitest 1488/1488; build+bundle guard green (entry 553.6/560, video code in the 12kB lazy chunk, ~2MB webm off-bundle); LIVE preview verified — constructed the overlay via the dev ESM module + a render pump, forceNonet active: 4 spirit videos + backdrop all readyState 4 + playing + currentTime advancing & looping, screenshot shows all 4 masked living spirits framing a legible board over the dusk backdrop, spirits[] empty (all 4 upgraded to video), 0 console errors. veo spend $2.10 (6 clips @ $0.35).

priorities.label: S96 P2 — combo order-symmetry bug (S93): Triangle->Spiral magic vs reverse plain line; ~14/30 orderings asymmetric
priorities.objective: Make 2-type combos order-independent (commutative) so e.g. Triangle->Spiral and Spiral->Triangle yield the same magic result.
priorities.scope: Locate the combo lookup table; make lookups canonical/order-insensitive (or fill the missing reverse entries). Tests for symmetry across all pairs. OUT: changing which combos are magic (only symmetry).
priorities.testing: tsc 0; vitest green + a new exhaustive symmetry test over all unordered type pairs.
priorities.check_method: S96 P2: ANALYZED, NOT auto-changed — correctly surfaced as a LOCKED-amend design decision rather than mechanically rewritten in an autonomous overnight session (INTEGRITY-WARNING + LOCKED protocol). FINDINGS: the combo table is INTENTIONALLY order-dependent (combos.ts:3 'Spec § V.1 LOCKED'; LOCKED_DECISIONS §6) and the order-dependence is asserted by THREE invariant tests (combos.test.ts:52 'at least one A->B differs from B->A', scoring.test.ts:126 Line->Dot is a placeholder, vortex.test.ts:125 Spiral->Dot is not a Vortex). It is load-bearing for scoring (MAGIC_BONUS premium + Filament trickle re-derived in carried->target order), discovery ('Combos N/14' HUD), and behavior helpers (Vortex/Filament/Anchor/Spindle). CRUCIALLY Triangle+Circle is a DELIBERATE dual-magic (Triangle->Circle=Wheel 3.0x vs Circle->Triangle=Star 2.0x) — a naive commutative fix would DELETE one of them. Symmetry map: 8 one-way-magic pairs (the real UX confusion), 1 dual-magic pair, 6 none, 4 magic self-pairs (= Magic-14). A commutative change would ~double magic occurrence -> inflate income -> break the S91-tuned ~157s match length (needs a rebalance). DELIVERABLE: docs/combo-order-symmetry-PDR.md — full symmetry map + 3 options (A full-commute / B mirror-the-8-keep-Wheel/Star / C keep-order-fix-UX) + impact analysis + recommendation (B+rebalance, or C as zero-risk). BLOCKED ON USER: pick option + (for B) confirm 'same magic both orders' + approve a rebalance. No code/spec changed; no tests broken; tsc/vitest still green.

priorities.label: S96 P3 — fix stale E2E v8->v9 assertion (smoke.spec.ts) + verify NONET 2-peer netcode
priorities.objective: Unblock the E2E lane (asserts PROTOCOL_VERSION 'v8' but it is 9 since S93) + reconfirm the host->client NONET snapshot round-trip is sound.
priorities.scope: Update the stale assertion to v9; re-run the sudokuSync round-trip unit coverage. OUT: a true 2-browser live playtest (needs the user / 2 peers).
priorities.testing: tsc 0; vitest green; grep confirms no remaining v8 assertion; (E2E full run needs a browser lane).
priorities.check_method: S96 P3: stale E2E protocol assertions fixed + NONET netcode reconfirmed. The S93 8->9 PROTOCOL_VERSION bump had broken TWO version tests in e2e/smoke.spec.ts, not just the one flagged: (1) 'Older-version joiner (v2)' asserted the host renders local 'v8' (now 9) -> changed to 'v9'; (2) 'Newer-version joiner (v9)' assumed host=v8 so a v9 peer was 'newer' — but the host IS v9 now, so a v9 joiner would MATCH (no rejection -> the waitForRejected would hang/fail) -> bumped the joiner override to v10 + assertions to 'v10' so it still exercises the peer>local 'your version is older' branch. Verified the message format (hostHandlers.formatProtocolMismatchMessage: 'Protocol mismatch (peer v{X}, you v{PROTOCOL_VERSION}). {advice}') + advice branches (peer<local -> other older; peer>local -> your version older) against source before editing. Confirmed via grep that no other stale v8/proto-8 assertions exist (remaining 'v8' hits are 'Pixi v8' lib refs). GATES: tsc 0; sudokuSync.test.ts 3/3 (NONET host->client snapshot round-trip sound); RAN the 2 version E2E tests headless -> BOTH PASS (2 passed, 20.8s) — real cross-browser WebRTC handshake + the corrected assertions. CI RESULT (run 27984400515): 32 passed / 1 failed — my two version tests PASS in CI (part of the 32); the 1 failure is a SEPARATE test 'Sym F — territorial hard-block' (smoke.spec.ts:356) that TIMED OUT (60s) on a WebRTC P2P flake (log flooded with net::ERR_ADDRESS_UNREACHABLE UDP drops). Timeout (not assertion mismatch) + no territorial/netcode change this session + videos lazy-loaded (never load in that test) => environmental flake, NOT a regression; likely greens on re-run. HONEST: my version-assertion fix is verified, but the E2E LANE is still red on this unrelated flaky test. OUT (still open): a true 2-PEER live NONET playtest (needs 2 real users) + the production hang trigger (single-browser-unreproducible; failure mode already eliminated S95).

reflexion_entries_to_archive: P1 #image-to-video-beats-procedural-twitch-for-character-life: the user rejected the S95 procedural breathe/blink/hop/float sprite idle as 'an early-2000s PowerPoint shape spinning'. The fix was NOT better procedural math — it was a real asset pipeline: composite each approved sprite onto a dark dusk keyframe (Pillow), veo-3.1 image-to-video it into a rich loop (breathing, blinking, lantern flicker, wing-flap, drifting fireflies), ffmpeg into a seamless opaque VP9, composite in Pixi with a feathered alpha mask. LESSONS: (a) when libvpx can't encode an alpha plane (this build silently dropped it on BOTH VP8 and VP9 even though alphamerge verified varying alpha pre-encode), don't fight the codec — pivot to render-time masking (opaque video + a feathered Pixi alpha-mask sprite), which is also more tunable + universal; (b) a perfect seamless loop comes free from xfade-against-self at offset=(D-CF) keeping the middle [CF,D] segment (first frame == last; measured seam diff 1.4/255); (c) de-risk generative spend by calibrating ONE clip (auth + cost + fidelity) before fanning out the rest in PARALLEL — 6 veo clips for $2.10, all useful; (d) verify generative motion empirically (videos readyState 4 + currentTime advancing/looping + live screenshot), not by trusting the prompt.

P2 #a-flagged-bug-can-be-a-locked-design-decision-in-disguise: the 'combo order-symmetry bug' looked like a quick table fix, but reading the code revealed it is INTENTIONALLY order-dependent (LOCKED § V.1 + 3 invariant tests asserting it) AND that Triangle->Circle=Wheel vs Circle->Triangle=Star is a DELIBERATE dual-magic a naive commutative fix would silently delete — plus it would ~double magic income and break the S91-tuned match length. LESSON: in an autonomous session, when 'the fix' would amend a LOCKED decision, break invariant tests, or change game balance with ambiguous intent, DON'T mechanically execute it — surface a decision-ready PDR (options + impact + recommendation) and let the awake user choose. The prior session had already flagged exactly this ('own PDR, LOCKED §6 amend'); honoring that beat barrelling ahead. 'Be pedantic' includes refusing to quietly gut a feature.

P3 #a-version-bump-breaks-more-tests-than-the-one-flagged: the handoff named only smoke.spec.ts:625 (assert 'v8') as stale after the S93 8->9 bump, but a SECOND test ('newer-version joiner v9') was also silently broken — it assumed host=v8 so a v9 peer was 'newer', but the host is now v9, so a v9 joiner MATCHES (no rejection -> waitForRejected would hang). LESSON: when a constant bumps, grep for ALL hardcoded references AND reason about tests whose PREMISE (not just literal) depends on the old value; the v9-newer test needed its peer bumped to v10 to still exercise the peer>local branch. Verified the fix by actually running the 2 E2E tests (2 passed, real WebRTC), not just by editing strings.

analyze_summary: S96 ANALYZE: autonomous overnight batch on explicit user full-batch pre-approval. Headline (P1) was a creative-engineering win — the user's complaint that the S95 procedural sprite idle 'looks like a PowerPoint shape spinning' was answered not with better math but with a real generative pipeline (Pillow keyframe -> veo-3.1 image-to-video -> ffmpeg seamless-loop+opaque-VP9 -> Pixi feathered-mask composite). Best moves: calibrated ONE veo clip before fanning out 5 in parallel (auth/cost/fidelity de-risked for $0.35), pivoted cleanly when libvpx couldn't encode an alpha plane (render-time Pixi mask instead of fighting the codec), kept it all off-bundle (entry flat 553.6/560) + video-primary-over-static-fallback for robustness, and VERIFIED empirically in the live preview (4 loops playing+looping+masked, board legible, spirits[] emptied). Highest-integrity call: P2 — recognized the 'combo order-symmetry bug' as a LOCKED §V.1 amendment that would delete the intentional Wheel/Star dual-magic and unbalance the S91-tuned economy, so produced a decision-ready PDR instead of mechanically rewriting locked semantics overnight (the 'urgency/LOCKED makes the gate MORE important' principle). P3 caught that the S93 protocol bump broke TWO version tests, not the one flagged, and verified the fix by actually running them (real WebRTC, 2 passed). Honest carry-forward: combo-symmetry awaits a user design pick; Voltkin live-recheck + true 2-peer NONET playtest remain user-side; optional P1 polish (parallax drift, veo-standard hero re-shoot) logged. No hazardous findings dropped. veo spend $2.10/$10.
